summary: The directed recommendation output can itself contain quadratically many rows in the number of distinct listening facts, and the deduplicated self-join query matches that worst-case output bound.

optimality.argument: With U nonfriend users all listening to the same three songs on one day, L = 3U distinct facts and the required output contains U(U-1) directed recommendations, which is Theta(L^2). The query deduplicates L facts, processes at most O(L^2) same-song/day user pairs, and performs one friendship exclusion phase over F rows.

replacement_checks.evidence: Three universally shared songs among U users force every ordered pair of distinct users into the output, yielding U(U-1) rows from only 3U distinct listening facts.

The self-join creates at most one row per ordered combination of two distinct listening facts after canonical user ordering; grouping, deduplication, and bidirectional projection do not exceed quadratic logical work.

Authored fixtures cover duplicate logs, exactly two versus three common songs, songs split across days, repeated qualification across days, existing friendships, independent pairs, bidirectional output, and empty input.
